title: Grammy Awards Record of the Year Odds & Predictions
text: The 68th Grammy Awards ceremony will take place on February 1, 2026, at Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles. Prediction market Kalshi has posted odds for the Record of the Year category, with three songs leading the competition. Kendrick Lamar - "luther": This track from Lamar's album GNX showcases collaboration with SZA and represents "the true testament to the behind-the-scenes art behind the vocals." The song credits six different producers, including Sounwave and Jack Antonoff, along with numerous engineers. Unlike other tracks on the album focusing on lyrical prowess, this entry emphasizes the production and engineering excellence behind the final recording. Rosé featuring Bruno Mars - "APT.": The debut single from Rosé's solo album achieved remarkable success for K-pop crossover music. It became "the fastest song by a female K-pop solo artist to surpass 100 million streams" on Spotify, accomplishing this feat within seven days. Mars contributed to both writing and production, along with providing vocals on the track. Lady Gaga - "Abracadabra": The leading single from Gaga's sixth studio album MAYHEM achieved international success, reaching number one in Lithuania and the top 10 in multiple countries including Austria, Brazil, Germany, and the UK. Critics have praised its overall production quality and songwriting craftsmanship. You can trade on these predictions at Kalshi, available in most U.S. states.
